postData.contentHtml:
A significant pronouncement from a former top Russian official suggests a profound shift in the country's political sentiment. The unnamed official has reportedly declared that Russia is 'over Putin,' indicating a growing weariness with the current leadership and a newfound ability to envision a future without President Vladimir Putin at the helm. This bold statement comes at a time when the Kremlin's grip on power appears to be facing internal pressures.

Adding weight to this observation are reports of a quiet exodus among Russia's elite. As the Kremlin reportedly moves to seize their assets, many influential figures are said to be abandoning the country. This trend points to a potential loss of faith in the stability and direction of the current regime, even among those who once benefited from its structures.

On social media, the sentiment surrounding the decline of Putin's support is largely positive, with discussions reflecting a mix of hope and cautious optimism. While some debate and skepticism exist, the prevailing mood suggests a readiness among a segment of the public to consider alternatives to the status quo. This evolving public discourse, coupled with the statements from former officials and the actions of the elite, paints a picture of a Russia grappling with its future.
postData.title: Cracks in the Kremlin: Former Official Declares Russia 'Over Putin' Amid Elite Exodus
postData.summary: A former high-ranking Russian official has made a striking declaration, stating that Russia is 'over Putin' and that a future without him is now conceivable. This comes as reports emerge of elites abandoning the country, with the Kremlin reportedly seizing their assets, signaling a potential shift in the nation's political landscape.

sources.title: Former top Russian official admits the country is over Putin and can imagine a future without him — even elites bail as Kremlin seizes their assets
